title: The bench that faces the wrong way
summary: In a city park, one bench faces away from the view, and a student and an old woman disagree about why.

body.speaker: Chloe
body.text: Excuse me. May I sit here?

body.speaker: Marta
body.text: Yes, please. It is a free bench.

body.speaker: Chloe
body.text: Thank you. But this is a strange bench. It faces the wall. All the other benches face the lake.

body.speaker: Marta
body.text: Yes. I know. I come here every day. This is my bench.

body.speaker: Chloe
body.text: Your bench? Why this one? The lake is so beautiful. Here you only see grey stone.

body.speaker: Marta
body.text: The lake is beautiful, yes. But everybody looks at the lake. When you look at the lake, you stop seeing people. You only see water.

body.speaker: Marta
body.text: On this bench, people walk in front of me. Old people, slow. Young people, fast. Mothers with babies. A man who is sad. A girl who is happy. The wall does not change. But the people always change.

body.speaker: Chloe
body.text: So you watch people, not the view.

body.speaker: Marta
body.text: The people are the view. I am eighty-one. My husband died. My friends died. But here, life walks past me all day. I am not alone on this bench.

body.speaker: Chloe
body.text: I think I will look at the lake today. But maybe, when I am older, I will understand your bench.

body.speaker: Marta
body.text: Maybe. Or maybe you will find your own wrong-way bench. Everybody needs one.

annotations.meaningVi: chiếc ghế quay sai hướng (cách nói riêng của bà)

grammarNotes.title: 'will' for future plans and predictions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'will + động từ nguyên thể' để nói về dự định hoặc dự đoán về tương lai. Trong bài: 'I think I will look at the lake today.' (Tôi nghĩ hôm nay tôi sẽ ngắm hồ.) và 'maybe you will find your own bench.' Sau 'will' luôn là động từ nguyên thể, không chia.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'will + base verb' for future plans or predictions. From the dialogue: 'I think I will look at the lake today.' and 'maybe you will find your own bench.' After 'will' the verb is always in its base form, never conjugated.

translationVi:
Chloe: Xin lỗi. Cháu ngồi đây được không ạ?
Marta: Được, mời cháu. Ghế này trống mà.
Chloe: Cháu cảm ơn. Nhưng đây là chiếc ghế kỳ lạ. Nó quay mặt vào tường. Tất cả ghế khác quay ra hồ.
Marta: Ừ. Bà biết. Ngày nào bà cũng đến đây. Đây là ghế của bà.
Chloe: Ghế của bà ạ? Sao lại là cái này? Hồ đẹp lắm. Ở đây bà chỉ thấy đá xám thôi.
Marta: Hồ đẹp, đúng. Nhưng ai cũng ngắm hồ. Khi cháu ngắm hồ, cháu thôi nhìn người ta. Cháu chỉ thấy nước.
Chloe: Cháu chưa hiểu.
Marta: Ở chiếc ghế này, người ta đi qua trước mặt bà. Người già, chậm. Người trẻ, nhanh. Mấy bà mẹ bồng con. Một người đàn ông buồn. Một cô gái vui. Bức tường không đổi. Nhưng con người thì luôn đổi.
Chloe: Vậy là bà ngắm người chứ không ngắm cảnh.
Marta: Con người chính là cảnh. Bà tám mươi mốt rồi. Chồng bà mất. Bạn bà mất. Nhưng ở đây, cuộc đời đi ngang qua bà suốt ngày. Bà không cô đơn trên chiếc ghế này.
Chloe: Cháu nghĩ hôm nay cháu sẽ ngắm hồ. Nhưng có lẽ, khi cháu già hơn, cháu sẽ hiểu chiếc ghế của bà.
Marta: Có lẽ. Hoặc có lẽ cháu sẽ tìm thấy chiếc ghế quay sai hướng của riêng mình. Ai cũng cần một cái.

questions.prompt: What is strange about Marta's bench?
options: It faces the wall, not the lake
questions.explanation: 'It faces the wall. All the other benches face the lake.'

questions.prompt: How often does Marta come to this bench?
What does Marta say happens when you look at the lake?
options: You stop seeing people and only see water
questions.explanation: 'When you look at the lake, you stop seeing people. You only see water.'

questions.prompt: What does Marta watch from her bench?
options: People walking past — old, young, mothers, a sad man, a happy girl
questions.explanation: 'people walk in front of me. Old people, slow. Young people, fast.'

questions.hint: When she talks about her family.

questions.prompt: Why is Marta not alone on the bench?
options: Because life walks past her all day
Because her husband sits with her
Because the lake talks to her
questions.explanation: 'here, life walks past me all day. I am not alone on this bench.'

questions.prompt: What does Chloe decide to do today?
questions.explanation: 'I think I will look at the lake today.'

questions.prompt: What does Marta mean by 'the people are the view'?
options: Watching people is more interesting to her than the scenery
questions.explanation: 'The people are the view.' — she prefers them to the lake.
questions.hint: Vocabulary in context, her short line.

questions.prompt: What does Marta say at the very end?
options: Everybody needs their own wrong-way bench
Chloe should never sit there again
The lake is the only good view
She will move to a new bench
questions.explanation: 'maybe you will find your own wrong-way bench. Everybody needs one.'

questions.prompt: What is the tone of this conversation?
options: Gentle and thoughtful, sharing a quiet life lesson
questions.explanation: Marta calmly explains a deeper way of seeing.

blanks.explanation: People walk in front of me.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Marta turned her bench away from the famous view to watch ordinary people instead. Is there a 'wrong-way' choice in your own life — paying attention to something others ignore — that gives you more than the obvious thing everyone else looks at?
